questions.text: Who is the author of the play 'The Browning Version'?
questions.explanation: Terence Rattigan is a famous British playwright known for this psychological drama.

questions.text: Taplow is a boy of which age?
questions.explanation: Taplow is a sixteen-year-old student in the lower fifth form.

questions.text: Why was Taplow at school on the last day of the term?
questions.explanation: He was called by Mr. Crocker-Harris to perform extra work because he missed a day due to illness.

questions.text: Who is Frank in the play?
questions.explanation: Frank is a young schoolmaster who teaches science.

questions.text: What is the name of the 'extra work' Taplow is doing?
questions.explanation: He is translating the Greek tragedy 'The Agamemnon' by Aeschylus.

questions.text: How does Taplow describe Mr. Crocker-Harris?
questions.explanation: Taplow uses these various descriptions to convey his master's cold and unemotional nature.

questions.text: What does Taplow say about Mr. Crocker-Harris's reaction to being liked?
questions.explanation: Taplow observes that Crocker-Harris seems to shrivel up even more if he thinks someone likes him.

questions.text: What is 'The Browning Version' actually referring to in the context of the full play?
options: A translation of a Greek play
questions.explanation: It refers to Robert Browning's translation of 'The Agamemnon'.

questions.text: Which subject does Taplow prefer?
questions.explanation: Taplow tells Frank he is interested in Science rather than 'muck' like the Agamemnon.

questions.text: What does Frank envy about Mr. Crocker-Harris?
options: The effect he has on students
questions.explanation: Frank is envious of the 'extraordinary' fear/respect students have for Crocker-Harris.

questions.text: Who is Millie?
questions.explanation: Millie Crocker-Harris is the wife of the strict schoolmaster.

questions.text: What is the 'remove' mentioned in the text?
questions.explanation: In British schools, a 'remove' is a division or form in the school system.

questions.text: Why did Millie tell Taplow to go to the chemist?
questions.explanation: She gives him a prescription to take to the chemist to release him from the anxiety of waiting for her husband.

questions.text: Does Mr. Crocker-Harris beat the students?
options: No, he is not a sadist
questions.explanation: Taplow clarifies that though he is strict, he is not a 'sadist' who gets pleasure from pain.

questions.text: What happened when Crocker-Harris made a joke in class?
options: Only Taplow laughed out of politeness
questions.explanation: Taplow laughed even though he didn't understand the Latin joke, just to be polite.

questions.text: What does Taplow do when Millie arrives?
options: He is terrified she heard him
questions.explanation: Taplow is frightened that she might have heard him imitating her husband.

questions.text: Where had Mr. Crocker-Harris gone?
questions.explanation: Millie mentions that her husband is at the Bursar's and might be there some time.

questions.text: The play is set in a:
questions.explanation: The scene takes place in a schoolroom where Taplow is waiting.

questions.text: What does Frank suggest Taplow should do since the master is late?
questions.explanation: Frank suggestively tells him to go and play golf, though Taplow is too scared to do so.

questions.text: Mr. Crocker-Harris is also known as:
questions.explanation: The students refer to him by the nickname 'The Crock'.

questions.text: How does Taplow imitate Crocker-Harris?
options: In a thin, slow, high-pitched voice
questions.explanation: Taplow tries to mimic the peculiar, cold tone of his teacher.

questions.text: What is the main theme of the play excerpt?
questions.explanation: The play explores the complex emotions students feel toward different types of teachers.

questions.text: Taplow says Crocker-Harris has no _______ inside him.
questions.explanation: He feels that the teacher is devoid of human emotions.

questions.text: What was Millie carrying when she entered?
questions.explanation: She enters with a shopping basket, having just come from outside.

questions.text: Why does Taplow still like 'The Crock' despite everything?
options: Because he is fair and unique
questions.explanation: Taplow admits that despite his coldness, he can't help liking him.

questions.text: What does Crocker-Harris tell Taplow about his results?
options: He got exactly what he deserved
questions.explanation: The master says he has given him exactly what he merits—no less and certainly no more.

questions.text: Frank's attitude towards Taplow's imitation is:
questions.explanation: Frank seems to enjoy the gossip and encourages Taplow to repeat the imitation.

questions.text: What does Taplow call the Greek play?
questions.explanation: He calls it 'muck' because of the way it is taught and the focus on grammar.

questions.text: Millie is described as being ________ than her husband.
questions.explanation: She is described as a thin woman in her late thirties who was more smartly dressed than the average schoolmaster's wife.

questions.text: Taplow's future depends on:
questions.explanation: He is waiting to hear if he has been promoted to the next form (his 'remove').

questions.text: Crocker-Harris is leaving the school to:
questions.explanation: He is leaving the school at the end of the term.

questions.text: What is Frank's reaction to Taplow saying he wants to take Science?
questions.explanation: Frank jokingly comments that the science department gets all the 'slackers'.

questions.text: Who says: 'I believe the Agamemnon's a masterpiece'?
questions.explanation: Frank admits the play itself is good, even if Taplow doesn't like it.

questions.text: How many characters appear on stage in this excerpt?
questions.explanation: Taplow, Frank, and Millie appear. Mr. Crocker-Harris is discussed but does not appear in this scene.

questions.text: What is the tone of the play?
questions.explanation: It is a serious play focused on human character and relationships.

questions.text: Taplow's reaction to the prospect of his master having feelings is:
questions.explanation: He finds the idea of the cold master having emotions quite disturbing.

questions.text: Mr. Crocker-Harris was specialized in which subject?
questions.explanation: He is a Classics master (Latin/Greek).

questions.text: What was the result of Taplow's illness?
options: He had to do extra work
questions.explanation: Missing one day due to illness resulted in him being called for extra work on the last day.

questions.text: The phrase 'cut' means to:
questions.explanation: In school slang, to 'cut' means to skip a class or session.

questions.text: Does Taplow go to the chemist in the end?
options: He goes to play golf instead
questions.explanation: He accepts Millie's suggestion and leaves to run the errand.
